summary: Backend-only testing of BrokerSaab User Management (Module 1) — Set/Login Password, Profile Edit, Avatar Upload, Contact Packs, Contact Unlocks. Authored /app/backend/tests/test_user_mgmt.py with 15 tests covering all requested endpoints; ALL 15 PASS. Regression suite (test_brokersaab_api.py) also passes for the User Management module-unrelated tests; 6 pre-existing failures stem from earlier iteration's non-idempotent advisor_signup test (advisor 9888888880 already in DB from iteration 1 → STATE['new_advisor_id'] never set on rerun). Not a backend defect.

minor.issue: Size validation uses len(raw) > 2_500_000 on the base64 string (incl. data URI prefix). Works, but reports max as '~1.8MB' in error message which conflicts with the actual ~2.5MB string cap. Cosmetic only — test passes.
No upper-bound on password length; bcrypt silently truncates passwords > 72 bytes — long passwords would later fail to authenticate. Consider validating max length 72.

Always returns 400 'Invalid credentials' even for non-existent user — good for security but indistinguishable from 'password not set yet'. Mobile UX may want a 'set password first' hint after OTP login.
The 'first-time free' is per-client lifetime (count_documents on contact_unlocks). Behaviour matches spec; just flagging it is unrecoverable — once used, free is gone forever.

action_items: (Optional) Cap password length to 72 bytes in /auth/password/set to avoid bcrypt silent truncation.
(Optional) Make 'upload avatar' error message match the actual byte cap ('max ~2.5MB of base64' or compute MB from cap).

(Pre-existing) Refactor /app/backend/tests/test_brokersaab_api.py advisor_signup to either delete prior TEST advisor or login via OTP flow so reruns don't cascade-fail 6 dependent tests. (Documented in iteration_1.json already.)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: bcrypt imported inside the request handlers (lines 339, 350) — works but a top-level import would shave a few ms and surface ImportError on boot. requirements.txt should pin bcrypt.
Password & avatar endpoints share /auth/* and /users/* prefixes — consistent with existing style.

POST /api/packs/buy does NOT actually debit user wallet (only inserts a wallet_transactions row with amount=-299 and creates the subscription). User.wallet_balance is untouched. Spec said 'wallet debited 299' — this means transaction is *logged* but balance unchanged. Confirmed by manual check: buying pack does not decrement /auth/me wallet_balance. **Likely a real bug** — main agent should review the intent: either (a) call adjust_wallet to deduct 299 OR (b) the pack is paid via Razorpay (mocked) and only logged for audit. Currently it's a half-implementation. My test only asserts the txn row exists (per the request wording 'wallet debited 299, … transaction logged') so the test passes, but flagging here as code review.
Unlock endpoint correctly atomically decrements credits_remaining via $inc on subscription doc. Good.
No race-condition guard if two unlock requests fire concurrently before count_documents check returns 0 — both could be treated as free. Low risk in mobile UX, but noted.
JWT_SECRET still hardcoded (21 bytes) — see iteration_1.
All Pydantic models present and used. Response data excludes _id via PROJ. Good.

success_rate.frontend: not tested this iteration (backend-only per request)

seed_data_creation: Each test run creates 2 fresh clients with random 10-digit phones starting with 98. These persist in DB but never collide across runs (random suffix). No advisor data created. Test data is prefixed 'TEST_UserMgmt'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_user_mgmt.py is fully re-runnable (random phones per run). test_brokersaab_api.py has 6 cascade failures on every rerun due to non-idempotent advisor_signup — to fix, either drop user with phone 9888888880 before run or restructure that test to look up the existing TEST advisor by phone and reuse. Backend URL read from /app/frontend/.env EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL.
rca_of_issue: Code-review finding (not test failure): POST /api/packs/buy logs a wallet_transactions debit row of -299 but does NOT call adjust_wallet() to deduct the user's wallet_balance. As a result, /auth/me wallet_balance does not change after purchase. Reproduction: register fresh client (wallet 500) → POST /api/packs/buy {pack_id:'pack_20'} → GET /api/auth/me shows wallet_balance still 500. If the intent is wallet-based payment, server.py line ~745 should call `await adjust_wallet(user['id'], -pack['price'], 'DEBIT', sub_id, ...)` instead of (or in addition to) the direct insert. Otherwise the txn log will be inconsistent with the user's actual balance and clients can buy unlimited packs without spending money.
